name: Canggu - Berawa (峇里島 - 貝拉瓦 / Pantai Berawa / Berawa Beach)
gps_location: -8.6649, 115.1379 (https://maps.app.goo.gl/Berawa+Beach+Canggu Pantai Berawa, in front of Finns Beach Club)
distance_from_dps_airport_km: 18 km (approx. 55-75 min by car depending on traffic)
distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu (Batu Bolong center): 5-10 min by scooter; From Uluwatu: 55-85 min by scooter
wave_type: Mixed beach break + reef break - three distinct peaks: 'The Peak' (A-frame beach break, mid tide), 'The Ledge' (shallow reef right-hander, low tide, advanced), 'The Bombie' (soft outside peak, SUP/foil/big-day spot). Predominantly left and right peaks.
skill_level: Beginner (Bombie/inside) to Intermediate (The Peak) to Advanced (The Ledge). Considered one of the best intermediate spots in Canggu.
wave_height_range: 2-8 ft (works 1-8 ft+, sweet spot 3-6 ft / head-high)
bottom_type: Sand on the inside, reef on the outside (mixed sand + reef); rocky outside sections
best_season: Dry season April-October (offshore SE/E trades and consistent SW swell). Wet season (Nov-Mar) still rideable but onshore winds frequent.
best_tide: Low to mid tide for The Peak and The Ledge; Bombie works best on higher tide and bigger swells. Different peaks light up at different tides, making Berawa surfable on most tide stages.
best_wind: Offshore from East / Southeast / Northeast (E-SE trade winds optimal); onshore by 10-11am typical
best_swell_direction: SW (Southwest) primary; also S and SSW
swell_consistency_rating: 4/5 (consistent but more swell-direction-dependent than Batu Bolong; quality varies with sandbar shifts)
wave_length_meters: 50-120 m on The Peak rights; shorter on The Ledge (~30-50 m) [uncertain]
wave_speed_rating: Medium (faster than Batu Bolong, slower than Echo Left; The Peak offers punchy walls, The Ledge is fast and shallow)
takeoff_zone_difficulty: Easy to moderate at The Peak (forgiving takeoff, room to spread out); difficult at The Ledge (steep, shallow reef drop); easy at the Bombie outside (soft rolling peaks).
secondary_peaks: Three distinct peaks: The Peak (main A-frame), The Ledge (south-side shallow reef), The Bombie (outside reef peak on bigger swells). Lineup spreads over shifting sandbanks, giving multiple takeoff zones.
peak_crowd_level: 3/5 to 4/5 - busier than it used to be due to Finns/Atlas beach club traffic but still feels less crowded than Batu Bolong/Echo. Peak crowd 7-11am dry season. Lineup spreads out across peaks.
access: Walk-in from Jalan Pantai Berawa, through the Finns Beach Club / Atlas Beach Fest / Komune Beach Club access points. Multiple beach entry paths via beach club walkways.
paddle_out_method: Paddle straight out from the beach over the inside sandbar; pick your peak (The Peak in front of Finns, The Ledge south end, Bombie outside on bigger days). On bigger swells use a rip channel between peaks for an easier paddle.
entry_fee: No beach entry fee. Parking: ~IDR 5,000 for car, IDR 2,000-3,000 for scooter. Some beach club lots charge IDR 10,000-20,000.
recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-7:30am) for offshore and uncrowded conditions; sunset session for golden-hour vibes (often onshore but rideable).
best_time_of_day: Before 8am for clean offshore conditions; mid-week mornings less crowded than weekends.
nearby_warung: Warung Bu Mong, La Baracca, Crate Cafe, Shady Shack nearby; major beach clubs (Finns Beach Club, Atlas Beach Fest, Komune, Manhattan) line the beach with restaurants/bars.
alternative_spot_within_10min: Batu Bolong (5-10 min north for softer waves), Echo Beach (10-15 min north for heavier reef), Seseh / Pererenan (15 min north when Canggu crowded), Petitenget (5 min south on smaller days).
hazards: Rip currents along the beach (notably near the river mouth/lagoon at south end), shallow reef on outside sections (The Ledge), occasional sharp rocks, urchins on reef, river-mouth runoff after rain, large beach club crowds in summer.
marine_hazards: Sea urchins on the outside reef sections (avoid The Ledge at low tide). Occasional jellyfish in wet season. No significant shark or stonefish reports. Bacterial water issues post-rain due to river/lagoon discharge.
reef_cut_severity_index: Low to Medium - sand-dominant inside means most wipeouts are safe; The Ledge reef can cause moderate cuts when surfed at low tide.
rip_current_pattern: Strong rip currents along the beach, especially near the lagoon/river mouth on the southern end and channels between sandbars. Useful for paddling out but dangerous for non-surfers. Stronger on outgoing tide and bigger swells.
lineup_etiquette: Standard surf etiquette; less crowded so generally more relaxed than Batu Bolong/Echo. Spread out across the three peaks; respect priority on takeoff. Smile, say hi to locals, don't snake.
localism_intensity: Low to Medium - generally welcoming for intermediate visitors. Some locals dominate The Ledge but overall vibe is mellow.
respect_protocol: Be courteous, don't crowd locals on The Ledge, respect rotation at The Peak. Don't bring beach club drinks/glassware onto the sand. Avoid surfing right in front of beach club zones during ceremonies/events.
resident_local_legends: Several Canggu/Berawa-based surfers shred The Peak regularly (e.g., Bali shortboard crew, local pros from nearby Canggu shaping community); fewer documented legends than Batu Bolong or Uluwatu. [uncertain]
religious_sensitivity: Small Hindu temples and shrines along the beach access; respect canang sari offerings. Observe Nyepi (Day of Silence in March) - no surfing, beach closed. Avoid disrupting temple ceremonies (melasti) on the beach.
medical_nearby: Penta Medical Canggu (24h clinic on Jl. Raya Canggu), Canggu Medical Clinic, BIMC Hospital Nusa Dua (major), Siloam Hospital Denpasar, Bali International Medical Centre.
nearest_clinic_distance_km: Penta Medical Canggu ~2 km; BIMC Nusa Dua ~22 km; Siloam Denpasar ~14 km
rescue_jet_ski_available: No dedicated rescue jet ski; Finns Beach Club has lifeguards on the beach during operating hours; Balawista informal coverage.
emergency_contact: Bali Emergency: 112; Balawista lifeguards: +62 361 755660; Police: 110; Ambulance: 118
nearest_surf_school: Several surf schools cluster in Berawa: Surf School Canggu Berawa, Wavehouse Bali, Planet Surf Camps Berawa, Bali Surf School Berawa. Lessons IDR 350,000-500,000 per 2-hour session.
board_rental_availability: Abundant - rental stalls along beach access road and within beach clubs; longboards, foamies, mid-lengths, shortboards available
surf_photographer_available: Yes - regular land-based photographers shoot from the beach and water; photos delivered same day via Instagram/QR code.
photographer_recommendation: Local Berawa/Canggu photographers; typical pack IDR 150,000-300,000 for edited photos. [uncertain]
accommodation_options: Wide range: COMO Uma Canggu (luxury), The Haven Suites, Bisma Eight, Tugu Bali, plus many guesthouses and villas. Surf camps include Planet Surf Camp Canggu/Berawa, Wavehouse Bali, Mondo Surf Village (Pererenan). Walking distance to break.
drone_allowed: Generally tolerated for surf photography; DGCA registration required for >250g drones in Indonesia. Avoid flying over beach clubs without permission, low over crowded lineup, or during religious ceremonies.
coworking_nearby: Yes - Tropical Nomad (Jl. Pantai Berawa, walking distance), Outpost Canggu (~10 min scooter), B Work Bali (Nelayan), Dojo Bali; multiple options within 5-15 min.
avg_lesson_price: IDR 400,000-550,000 (~USD 26-36 / TWD 800-1,100) per 2-hour group lesson; private IDR 600,000-900,000
board_rental_daily: Half-day: IDR 75,000-150,000; full-day IDR 100,000-200,000; performance shortboard up to IDR 200,000-250,000
water_quality_rating: Dry season (May-Oct): Good to Fair. Wet season (Nov-Mar): Poor after heavy rain due to river/lagoon runoff at south end; ear/skin infection risk. Avoid surfing 24-48h post-downpour.
annual_competition_window: Occasional regional contests and surf school competitions; sometimes hosts amateur/grom events. No major WSL CT event. [uncertain]
community_events: Beach club events at Finns / Atlas / Komune; Trash Hero Canggu and 4Ocean beach clean-ups; Deus Canggu 9ft & Single Festival activities nearby; Sunday DJ sessions; full moon parties.
